mission.title: Install and Configure Strimzi Kafka Operator
mission.description: Strimzi Kafka Operator allows you to run Apache Kafka on Kubernetes, providing a way to manage Kafka clusters easily. It is ideal for users looking to deploy Kafka in a Kubernetes environment with features like scaling, monitoring, and configuration management. Key trade-offs include the complexity of managing Kafka's distributed nature and the need for persistent storage.

steps.title: Add Strimzi Helm Repository
steps.description:
This step adds the Strimzi Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration, allowing you to install the Kafka Operator.

```bash

helm repo add strimzi https://strimzi.io/charts/

helm repo update

```

> This command adds the official Strimzi Helm repository.
> Updates the local Helm chart repository cache.

steps.title: Install the Strimzi Kafka Operator
steps.description:
This command installs the Strimzi Kafka Operator in the 'kafka' namespace using the specified version.

```bash

kubectl create namespace kafka

helm install strimzi strimzi/strimzi-kafka-operator --namespace kafka --version 0.44.0

```

> Creates a namespace for the Kafka Operator.
> Installs the Strimzi Kafka Operator.

steps.title: Verify the Installation
steps.description:
Check if the Strimzi Kafka Operator pods are running correctly in the 'kafka' namespace.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n kafka

```

> This command lists all pods in the 'kafka' namespace to verify the installation.

steps.title: Create a Kafka Cluster
steps.description:
This step creates a Kafka cluster using a custom resource definition (CRD).

```bash

cat <<EOF | kubectl apply -f -
apiVersion: kafka.strimzi.io/v1beta2
kind: Kafka
metadata:
  name: my-cluster
  namespace: kafka
spec:
  kafka:
    version: "3.4.0"
    replicas: 3
    listeners:
      plain:
        port: 9092
        authentication:
          type: tls
    storage:
      type: ephemeral
  zookeeper:
    replicas: 3
    storage:
      type: ephemeral
EOF

```

> Creates a Kafka cluster named 'my-cluster'.

cat <<EOF | kubectl apply -f -

  name: my-cluster

          type: tls
    storage:
      type: ephemeral
  zookeeper:
    replicas: 3
    storage:
      type: ephemeral
EOF

steps.title: Verify the Kafka Cluster
steps.description:
Check if the Kafka cluster is running and ready.

```bash

kubectl get kafka -n kafka

```

> This command lists all Kafka clusters in the 'kafka' namespace.

uninstall.title: Uninstall the Strimzi Kafka Operator
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Strimzi Kafka Operator and all associated resources. Be aware that this will delete the Kafka cluster and all data associated with it.

```bash

helm uninstall strimzi --namespace kafka

kubectl delete namespace kafka

```

> Uninstalls the Strimzi Kafka Operator.
> Deletes the 'kafka' namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade the Strimzi Kafka Operator
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you have a backup of your Kafka data. This step will upgrade the Strimzi Kafka Operator to a newer version.

```bash

kubectl get kafka -n kafka -o yaml > kafka-backup.yaml

helm upgrade strimzi strimzi/strimzi-kafka-operator --namespace kafka --version 0.44.0

kubectl get pods -n kafka

```

> Backs up the current Kafka cluster configuration.
> Upgrades the Strimzi Kafka Operator.
> Verifies that the new pods are running.
commands: kubectl get kafka -n kafka -o yaml > kafka-backup.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to pull image'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The specified image for the Kafka broker is not available.

**Fix:**
Check the image tag in your Kafka CR and ensure it exists.

troubleshooting.title: Kafka cluster is not starting.
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** Insufficient resources allocated to the Kafka pods.

**Fix:**
Increase the resource limits in the Kafka CR and redeploy.

troubleshooting.title: Zookeeper is not reachable.
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** Network policies or firewall rules are blocking access.

**Fix:**
Check network policies and ensure Zookeeper ports are open.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Kafka broker not ready'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Kafka broker pods are not in a running state.

**Fix:**
Check the logs of the Kafka pods for errors using 'kubectl logs <pod-name> -n kafka'.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for Kafka 3.4.0, improved metrics collection.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: Update your Kafka CR to use the new version and redeploy.

resolution.summary: You should see the Kafka cluster listed with 'kubectl get kafka -n kafka' showing 'my-cluster' in the output.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with sufficient resources.
